title: Repair the exact config-source blocker and rerun the single-device continue-onboarding path
releaseGate: True next PRD batch justified: the closure-stage validation question is already answered, and the owner wants the resident topic to continue. Use this batch only for the exact product repair and same-path rerun; do not reuse it for runtime/session repair or passive waiting.

stories.title: Repair the exact config-source blocker on single-device onboarding ensure
stories.scope: Fix the root cause of the live single-device continue-onboarding blocker `onboarding config source is required` on the same `plan -> ensure` path used by `DeviceV2ManagementRedesign.vue`. The repair must make `POST /device/v2/:code/onboarding/ensure` return a normal onboarding payload instead of `data=null` for the repaired path, without dropping the controller-backed evidence fields already surfaced by `GET /onboarding`.
nonGoals: Do not close with docs-only or evidence-only edits.
Do not widen into batch onboarding or unrelated environment repair.
Do not hide a remaining contract blocker behind generic success wording.
acceptance: The repaired single-device `ensure` path no longer fails specifically because the config source is missing, or the blocker file names the exact remaining file/function/contract gap.

If the repair succeeds, the path still preserves or surfaces the exact onboarding evidence fields needed for the rerun: `actions`, `task_id`, `store_run_id`, `controller_stage`, `store_run_status`, `core_store_status`, and `manageable_status`.
notes: Must read first: docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-024.md, docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-025.md, and docs/development/device-v2-onboarding-observability/10-阶段结论-OneOPS_DeviceV2纳管观测_v0.1.md.
Keep the fix anchored to the same single-device continue-onboarding flow that already produced the exact blocker.
Not enough for DONE: changing only docs, changing only evidence wording, or rerunning without addressing the config-source contract.

stories.title: Rerun one exact single-device continue-onboarding probe after the repair
stories.scope: After `D2ON-026`, run exactly one bounded live probe on the same single-device `plan -> ensure -> GET /onboarding` path. Capture the exact `ensure` response and the returned controller-backed tuple without paraphrasing blocked or failed outcomes as success.
nonGoals: Do not switch to batch onboarding.
Do not omit the exact ensure payload shape or the exact controller tuple.
Do not treat a stale previous GET result as the new rerun verdict.
acceptance: The evidence records the device code, request bodies, exact `ensure` response, and whether a `monitor_controller_stage` action was returned during the rerun path.
The evidence preserves the returned `controller_stage`, `store_run_status`, `core_store_status`, and `manageable_status` values explicitly for success, failed, blocked, or unknown outcomes.
notes: Must read first: docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-024.md, docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-026.md, and the exact files changed by D2ON-026.
Prefer the same device code used in D2ON-024 when it is still valid; if not, record the exact replacement code and why it changed.
Not enough for DONE: a prose summary without the exact ensure payload and controller tuple.

stories.title: Update readiness after the repaired rerun with an explicit program verdict
stories.scope: Update readiness and review only after `D2ON-027` yields the repaired rerun verdict. State explicitly whether the program is now high-quality complete with a successful product result, high-quality complete with an exact blocker, or still needs another execution repair round.
nonGoals: Do not fall back to another passive keep-open note.
Do not claim monitor/log success without the rerun evidence.
Do not close the resident topic with STOP unless the owner explicitly asks for it.
acceptance: `docs/prd-autodev/device-v2-onboarding-observability/evidence/final-readiness.md` and `docs/prd-autodev/device-v2-onboarding-observability/review.md` are updated from the rerun result.
The next-step note explicitly says whether another execution repair batch is required.
notes: This story closes the new repair round honestly; it must not repeat the doc-only drift that forced batch-008.
If the rerun still returns an exact blocker, preserve the exact tuple and say whether that blocker justifies another repair round.
